patient.query: Yeah, it feels like I’m just stuck in this endless loop. I keep thinking about all my friends who seem to have their lives together, and then there's me, feeling like I'm not doing enough. The pressure I put on myself to figure it out just adds to everything, you know?

I'm stuck in an endless loop

patient.query: I guess it just feels like I’m not making progress. I see people getting promotions or starting their own businesses, and here I am still trying to find a way to make my current job work. I sometimes wake up and wonder if I’m just wasting my time or if I’m even cut out for this field at all. It’s exhausting.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not making progress
I'm wasting my time
I'm not cut out for this field

patient.query: Yeah, it’s hard because I have these moments where I think I’m doing okay, but then I check social media or talk to someone, and it feels like everyone else is ahead of me. I start doubting my skills, like maybe I’m just not good enough. Sometimes I get this pit in my stomach thinking I’ll be stuck forever.

maybe I’m just not good enough
I’ll be stuck forever

It feels like everyone’s moving forward while I’m still figuring out how to get out of my own way. It can be so frustrating, especially when I thought I had a plan, and now it feels like everything’s falling apart.

patient.query: I guess I’m starting to wonder if I even chose the right path for myself. I used to feel pretty confident about my job, but now I’m not so sure. Sometimes I think maybe I should have pursued something completely different, something I was more passionate about. But then I feel scared to make any big changes, like what if I just end up worse off? It’s that fear of the unknown, I guess.

automatic_thoughts: maybe I should have pursued something different
what if I just end up worse off

Lately, I’ve been realizing that I’ve been settling too much, maybe thinking I could just make it work instead of really going after what I want. But then there's this voice in my head that says, "What if you fail?" or "What if it’s too risky?" It’s confusing, like I want to chase after something great, but I’m held back by all this

patient.query: Failure… I guess I imagine it looking like making a big leap and then just crashing and burning. Like, if I tried to switch jobs or start something new and it didn’t work out, I’d feel embarrassed, like I’d let myself down and everyone around me. It just plays into this dread I’ve got about not living up to expectations. I keep picturing how I'd have to explain it to others, and that just makes it worse.

I’d let myself down
I’d let everyone around me down
I might not live up to expectations

patient.query: I think it’s just the fear of judgment. Like, I worry people will think I’m not capable or that I’ve messed up somehow. It feels like there’s this pressure to show that I’ve got everything together, and if I don’t, it’s like I’m failing in front of everyone. I don’t want to be the one who seemed promising but then flopped. It's that feeling of being exposed and vulnerable, and honestly, it scares me.

automatic_thoughts: people will think I’m not capable
I’ve messed up somehow
I’m failing in front of everyone
I don’t want to be the one who flopped

I see people around me who seem to have it all figured out, and it feels like they’re just waiting to see if I’ll succeed or fail. It's like I’ve set this standard for myself that I’m terrified of not meeting—what if I let down the people who believe in me? I guess it’s this heavy
